target: eMAG detail HTML and detail-image generation

brand_trust_gif_banner.usage: brand familiarity, service cues, platform-like trust context
brand_trust_gif_banner.motion: gif allowed when it proves brand, usage, installation, or result

product_tech_hero_banner.usage: technical products where the buyer needs compatibility, safety, or feature confidence first

official_promo_reference_tiles.usage: reference for people/category mood only; do not reuse commercially unless rights are confirmed
official_promo_reference_tiles.html_form: source material for generated banner layouts, not direct proof content

banner_style_selector.prompt_anchor: dark premium background, real people or parcel scene, 3 service cues maximum, deterministic text overlay
product cutout, technical background, usage scene, compatibility/safety chips
bright marketplace blue, person plus category objects, rounded floating product tiles
avoid: direct eMAG logo use without permission

module_groups.label: 3-second result hook
module_groups.purpose: Open with the outcome the buyer wants, not a neutral product definition.

modules.buyer_question: What result do I get if I buy this?
modules.copy_rule: Lead with relief, solved scenario, or gained convenience; avoid price, discount, and exaggerated claims.
modules.visual_rule: Large product/lifestyle composition, one dominant headline, 2-3 proof chips only.

modules.buyer_question: Which annoying situation does this remove?
modules.copy_rule: Name the buyer's frustration, then connect one feature to one practical relief.
modules.visual_rule: Before/after scene, simple icon pair, or color-banded contrast panel.
modules.html_form: single image board plus short paragraph

module_groups.label: Buyer-question flow
module_groups.purpose: Replace the buyer's mental objections with explicit answers in page order.

modules.buyer_question: Why should I believe this feature matters?
modules.copy_rule: Question title plus answer; each block explains one concern only.
modules.visual_rule: Use a small anchor symbol, bold question, then 2-4 lines of answer.
modules.html_form: text panel, color band, or image board

modules.buyer_question: What would the buyer otherwise go back to check?
modules.copy_rule: Use actual reviews, Q&A, competitor comments, or category questions; do not invent named customer claims.
modules.visual_rule: Plain white FAQ or light color-band list, no decoration-heavy layout.
modules.html_form: h3 plus p list or ul/li

module_groups.label: Visual hierarchy anchors
module_groups.purpose: Make long mobile detail pages scannable without relying on dense paragraphs.

modules.buyer_question: What are the main reasons to keep reading?
modules.copy_rule: Feature name first, benefit second, no more than 8 bullets per band.
modules.visual_rule: One restrained background color per band; no nested cards; clear left alignment.
modules.html_form: table-backed color band or generated image board

modules.buyer_question: What are the 3 highest-priority reasons to keep reading after the banner?
modules.copy_rule: Use exactly 3 short buyer-facing decision anchors. Each anchor must be one purchase role: fit/compatibility, result/control, safety/proof, scene relief, or trust. Do not write internal SOP explanations.
modules.visual_rule: Mobile-first stacked cards, full available width, very light background, one colored left rail per card, bold 01/02/03 title, 1-2 lines of support text. Keep it calmer than a banner and more readable than icon chips.
modules.html_form: blockquote stack or div stack with inline style; can degrade to p/strong blocks

modules.buyer_question: Where should my eye go first?
modules.copy_rule: Use a repeated marker only as a reading anchor, not as decoration.
modules.visual_rule: Small symbol, short bold label, then supporting text.
modules.html_form: inline text, light table row, or image board

module_groups.label: Brand and platform familiarity
module_groups.purpose: Build trust without repeating a logo as empty branding.

modules.buyer_question: Can I trust this brand and buying context?
modules.copy_rule: Use only platform/service claims that are true for the listing or seller; otherwise keep it to brand values and support process.
modules.visual_rule: Dark premium banner, real people or platform-style parcel scene, 3 service icons maximum.
modules.html_form: generated banner image or gif; avoid complex HTML

modules.buyer_question: Did real buyers have the same concern?
modules.copy_rule: Only quote or summarize real review data. If no review data exists, convert it to 'common questions' instead of fake testimonials.
modules.visual_rule: Dark background, review cards, star rating only when sourced.

module_groups.label: Decision facts
module_groups.purpose: Give the buyer enough concrete facts to decide without leaving the detail page.

modules.copy_rule: Use source specs only; translate units consistently.
modules.visual_rule: Two-column table, light zebra rows, strong labels.

modules.buyer_question: What arrives and how do I use it correctly?
modules.copy_rule: List package contents and usage cautions factually.
modules.visual_rule: Product flat lay, numbered usage steps, concise labels.
